sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated nutrition or symptom probability model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MCM6/LCT rs4988235 CT call is available for lactase-persistence context. This is not a diagnosis of lactose intolerance.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This marker can provide lactose-related context, but symptoms, ancestry, diet history, and non-genetic causes also matter.

nutritionContext: Use this report as educational nutrition-genetics context, not as a diet or supplement plan.
Track symptoms and dietary history with a clinician or registered dietitian if lactose tolerance is a concern.
Do not change foods, supplements, or medical care based on genotype alone.

references.title: Nutritional Genomics and Direct-to-Consumer Genetic Testing

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated nutrition probability model is supplied.
Other nutrition-related genetic and non-genetic contributors are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Nutrition traits and symptoms can be influenced by genetics, ancestry, diet history, environment, gut health, and other non-genetic factors.
A single marker should not be used to diagnose lactose intolerance or prescribe a diet.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not diagnose lactose intolerance, prescribe a diet, or recommend supplements.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
